episode_text:
day=1; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Social with person in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=open mouth; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is cold-served fruit or dessert AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is social or personal THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed meal was strawberries with whipped cream and the user selected no reheating, which is consistent with cold foods. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal object in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected this in a front-social context, indicating sensitivity to blocking the forward view in socially relevant situations. No other directional variation observed yet.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected a slow robot speed in a social meal, consistent with comfort and social acceptability. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this for strawberries, favoring reliable bite acquisition. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, suggesting a preference for verification before transfer in a socially visible setting. This is also compatible with the user's ability to use the web interface through assistive access. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer less compatible with physical ability; the observed choice also supports inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user selected inside-mouth transfer and outside-mouth distance does not apply. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling to indicate readiness to initiate transfer, suggesting a preference for a quiet nonverbal cue in the observed social setting. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press physical buttons, and the observed feeding preference used open-mouth readiness despite the social context. This indicates that physical-effort constraints currently outweigh possible social ambiguity concerns. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any drink-related task THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press physical buttons, and the observed drinking preference used open-mouth readiness. This is the strongest currently supported low-effort initiation method compatible with the user's abilities. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any mouth-wiping task THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press physical buttons, and the observed wiping preference used open-mouth readiness. This reflects a low-effort initiation style compatible with the user's motor limitations. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food or drink or wiping task THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling when the tool reached transfer position, indicating preference for nonverbal completion cues in the observed context. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected sensor-based completion detection for bites, which is reliable and avoids requiring button presses that the user cannot reliably perform. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is social AND food condition is any drink-related task THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception and button input less suitable; the observed choice was autocontinue. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is social AND food condition is any mouth-wiping task THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and cannot reliably perform head gestures, making autocontinue the most compatible observed option. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting between bites in a context where preserving the forward view appears important, consistent with minimizing prolonged obstruction. No other context-dependent variation observed yet.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed meal included whipped cream and the user selected less dipping, suggesting a preference to limit messiness in a social setting. IF food has no dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food or drink or wiping task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate autocontinue wait, indicating a balance between allowing time to respond and avoiding excessive delay. No context-dependent variation observed yet.
